//: Pylance/Pyright project config. Editor-only — CI uses ruff + mypy (see pyproject.toml). Purpose: resolve imports against THIS project's .venv and the src/ layout for EVERY file at once, so Pylance never reports phantom 'could not be resolved' on installed deps.
// extraPaths: src/ layout — packages live under src/ (import oneops...). Without this, first-party imports don't resolve.
// typeCheckingMode: off — the CI type checker is MYPY (pyproject + ci.sh), not Pyright. Pylance should resolve imports and catch syntax/undefined-name, NOT impose its own (stricter, divergent) type layer that flags 100+ 'errors' the mypy gate passes. This keeps the editor consistent with CI. Run mypy for type checking.
// reportInvalidTypeForm: off-mode artifact: with type-checking off Pyright can't resolve type names (e.g. a Protocol/TYPE_CHECKING import) so it mislabels valid types as 'variables'. mypy validates these correctly in CI, so silence the Pyright-only false positives.
